narrative.us_narrative: US markets rallied on stronger-than-expected GDP data, with tech leading gains as NVIDIA surged 4.2% on AI infrastructure demand. The S&P 500 posted its best day in two weeks.
narrative.asia_narrative: Asian markets were mixed overnight. Japan's Nikkei gained 0.8% on yen weakness while Hong Kong fell 0.3% on renewed property sector concerns.

world_news.headline: EU Announces Sweeping New Sanctions on Russian Energy Exports
world_news.summary: The European Union has agreed on its most comprehensive sanctions package targeting Russian LNG exports, affecting approximately €12 billion in annual trade. The move is expected to tighten global energy supply and could push European gas prices higher heading into the northern hemisphere winter.
world_news.aus_impact_note: Positive for Australian LNG exporters (WDS, STO) as European buyers seek alternative supply sources. Could support AUD via higher commodity prices.

world_news.headline: US-China Semiconductor Restrictions Escalate After Beijing Retaliatory Measures
world_news.summary: Beijing has announced export controls on gallium and germanium compounds in response to expanded US chip restrictions. The tit-for-tat measures raise risks of further technology decoupling between the world's two largest economies.
world_news.aus_impact_note: Risk of broader trade tensions could weigh on AUD and mining stocks if China growth concerns resurface. Watch lithium and rare earth miners.

world_news.headline: Reserve Bank of India Cuts Key Rate by 25bps in Surprise Move
world_news.summary: The RBI unexpectedly cut its repo rate to 5.75%, citing slowing domestic growth and benign inflation. The decision signals a more dovish tilt among Asian central banks and could influence RBA thinking on rates.

world_news.headline: UK and India Finalise Historic Free Trade Agreement
world_news.summary: After three years of negotiations, the UK and India have signed a comprehensive FTA covering goods, services, and digital trade. The deal is the UK's largest post-Brexit trade agreement and opens access to India's 1.4 billion consumer market.

world_news.headline: Middle East Ceasefire Talks Stall as Regional Tensions Persist
world_news.summary: UN-brokered ceasefire negotiations have hit an impasse over prisoner exchange terms. Oil markets remain on edge with Brent crude up 1.2% on supply disruption fears.
world_news.aus_impact_note: Higher oil prices supportive for energy stocks (WDS, STO) but negative for consumer inflation outlook.

world_news.headline: Brazil Central Bank Signals End to Tightening Cycle
world_news.summary: Brazil's BCB held rates steady at 13.25% and signalled the hiking cycle is complete, joining a growing list of emerging market central banks shifting to neutral or easing stances.

asx_deep_dive.overview: The ASX 200 extended its winning streak to four sessions, buoyed by commodity strength and a weaker AUD. Mining stocks led gains as iron ore prices hit a 3-month high on renewed China stimulus expectations, while tech names tracked the NASDAQ higher overnight.

sectors.name: Mining & Resources
sectors.summary: Best-performing sector as iron ore rallied 2.5% on Chinese steel demand optimism following PBoC comments on supporting the property sector.

movers.name: BHP Group
movers.explanation: BHP rose 2.3% as iron ore prices rallied on renewed China stimulus hopes after PBoC signalled further property sector support.

movers.name: Fortescue
movers.explanation: Fortescue surged 3.1%, outperforming peers on iron ore strength plus positive broker commentary on its green energy transition progress.

movers.name: South32
movers.explanation: South32 bucked the trend, falling 0.8% after reporting production disruptions at its Worsley alumina refinery.

sectors.name: Banks & Financials
sectors.summary: Banks were largely flat as investors await the RBA's next meeting. Macquarie outperformed on strong asset management inflows.

movers.name: Macquarie Group
movers.explanation: Macquarie gained 1.2% after reporting record Q4 asset management inflows of $18.2 billion, beating consensus estimates.

movers.name: CommBank
movers.explanation: CBA edged 0.3% lower on profit-taking after last week's rally to all-time highs. No stock-specific catalysts.

movers.name: NAB
movers.explanation: NAB rose modestly after an analyst upgrade from Morgan Stanley citing improving business lending margins.

sectors.name: Tech
sectors.summary: Tech names followed the NASDAQ higher, with WiseTech and Xero both gaining on no specific news.

movers.name: WiseTech Global
movers.explanation: WiseTech climbed 1.5% tracking NASDAQ gains and benefiting from continued momentum in global logistics digitalisation.

movers.name: Xero
movers.explanation: Xero gained 0.8% ahead of its quarterly update next week. Consensus expects continued subscriber growth in UK and US markets.

movers.name: Computershare
movers.explanation: Computershare dipped 0.4% as margin income expectations were tempered by falling US bond yields earlier in the week.

sectors.name: Healthcare
sectors.summary: Healthcare underperformed with CSL dragging the sector lower on currency headwinds from a weaker AUD.

movers.name: Pro Medicus
movers.explanation: Pro Medicus surged 2.1% after announcing a $28M contract with a major US hospital network for its Visage imaging platform.

movers.name: CSL Limited
movers.explanation: CSL fell 0.9% as the weaker AUD creates translation headwinds for its predominantly USD-denominated revenue.

movers.name: ResMed
movers.explanation: ResMed slipped 0.5% on continued GLP-1 obesity drug competition concerns despite strong underlying device demand.

sectors.name: Energy
sectors.summary: Energy stocks gained on higher oil and gas prices, with EU sanctions on Russian LNG providing a tailwind for Australian producers.

movers.name: Woodside Energy
movers.explanation: Woodside rose 1.8% on EU Russian LNG sanctions, positioning Australian LNG exporters as alternative suppliers to European buyers.

movers.name: Santos
movers.explanation: Santos gained 1.4% tracking higher Brent crude prices and benefiting from the same EU sanctions catalyst as Woodside.

movers.name: AGL Energy
movers.explanation: AGL was flat to slightly lower, underperforming upstream peers as it lacks direct LNG export exposure.

sectors.name: REITs / Property
sectors.summary: REITs were the worst-performing sector as bond yields ticked higher, reducing the relative attractiveness of property yields.

movers.name: Goodman Group
movers.explanation: Goodman Group bucked the REIT sell-off, gaining 0.3% on continued data centre development demand which offsets rate sensitivity.

movers.name: Scentre Group
movers.explanation: Scentre fell 1.2% as rising bond yields weigh on retail REIT valuations. Foot traffic data also showed softening in discretionary retail.

movers.name: GPT Group
movers.explanation: GPT dropped 0.9% with the broader REIT sector on higher bond yields. Office vacancy concerns continue to weigh on sentiment.

watchlist.name: Amcor
watchlist.news_digest: No significant news today. Trading within recent range ahead of Q3 results due mid-April.

watchlist.name: iShares S&P500 ETF
watchlist.news_digest: Tracking the S&P 500's modest overnight gain. ETF continues to see steady inflows from Australian retail investors.

watchlist.name: James Hardie
watchlist.news_digest: James Hardie slipped on disappointing US housing starts data released overnight, which fell 3.2% month-on-month — a headwind for building materials demand.

watchlist.name: Liontown Resources
watchlist.news_digest: Liontown surged on news that the Kathleen Valley lithium mine ramp-up is ahead of schedule, with first spodumene shipment confirmed for Q2. Volume significantly above average.

watchlist.name: Macquarie Group
watchlist.news_digest: Record Q4 asset management inflows of $18.2 billion beat consensus. Approaching 52-week highs with momentum from both MAM and banking divisions.

watchlist.name: Nickel Industries
watchlist.news_digest: Nickel Industries fell as LME nickel prices dropped to 3-month lows on Indonesian supply concerns. Market watching for any production guidance update.

watchlist.name: Pilbara Minerals
watchlist.news_digest: Pilbara rallied after the latest Battery Metals Exchange (BMX) auction cleared at a higher-than-expected price, signalling improving lithium demand. Analysts note the stock remains well below its 52-week high.

watchlist.name: Qualitas Real Estate
watchlist.news_digest: No significant news today. Light trading volume. Qualitas continues to build its commercial real estate debt fund pipeline.

watchlist.name: Telstra
watchlist.news_digest: No significant news today. Telstra trading in a narrow range as investors await the ACCC's final decision on the TPG network-sharing deal.

watchlist.name: Webjet
watchlist.news_digest: Webjet gained on broader travel sector strength. European and Asian travel bookings trending above pre-COVID levels according to latest IATA data.

watchlist.name: Woolworths
watchlist.news_digest: Woolworths edged lower as cost-of-living pressures continue to weigh on consumer discretionary spend. Analysts watching for any impact on Big W division margins.

watchlist.name: WCM Quality Global Growth
watchlist.news_digest: No significant news today. The LIC continues to trade at a modest discount to NTA. Portfolio benefiting from US tech exposure.

culture.title: Pharaoh: King of Ancient Egypt
culture.description: A major international loan exhibition from the British Museum featuring over 250 objects spanning 3,000 years of ancient Egyptian civilisation. Includes royal sculptures, jewellery, and papyri never before displayed in Australia.
